shortDescription: IRCC temporary resident visa · Tourism, family, business · Biometrics may apply

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current IRCC visitor visa fee (CAD $100) and biometrics fee on canada.ca before quoting clients.

alert.title: Purpose must match documents
alert.body: Tourism, family visit, and business visitor files need different evidence. Misaligned purpose is a common refusal reason—align invitation, itinerary, and cover letter.

about.label: Description
about.value: Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) allowing Indian nationals to visit Canada for tourism, family visits, or short business trips. Most applications are submitted online through the IRCC portal.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid passport · Genuine temporary purpose · Strong ties to India · Sufficient funds · Clean immigration history

about.label: Single vs multiple entry
about.value: Officer decides entry type and validity at approval. Do not promise multiple-entry unless client profile supports it.

about.label: After approval
about.value: TRV counterfoil in passport; client must respect stay duration and purpose. Overstay affects future Canada applications.

eligibility.note: Check expiry before booking VAC appointment
Itinerary, invitation letter, or business documents as applicable

eligibility.criterion: Proof of funds for trip duration
eligibility.note: Bank statements, ITR, sponsor letter if applicable
Book within 30 days of BIL if required
Based on residence history and stay plans

eligibility.criterion: No prior misrepresentation or serious inadmissibility

redFlagsBanner: If refused, do not reapply immediately with the same weak ties evidence. Address the specific refusal reason before a new submission.

redFlags.title: Weak home-country ties
redFlags.description: Unemployed applicant with no property, family, or business ties; vague return plan.
redFlags.fix: Document employment, business, property, family obligations; employer leave letter

redFlags.title: Insufficient or unseasoned funds
redFlags.description: Large recent deposits, funds not matching income, or no sponsor proof.
redFlags.fix: 4–6 month bank history; sponsor ITR + employment letter; explain large credits

redFlags.title: Purpose mismatch
redFlags.description: Tourism file with business documents only, or family visit without host invitation.
redFlags.fix: Align purpose, cover letter, itinerary, and supporting documents

redFlags.title: Previous refusals not addressed
redFlags.description: Canada or other country refusals hidden or not explained in cover letter.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure + changed circumstances + stronger evidence

redFlags.title: Overstated stay or vague itinerary
redFlags.description: Long stay without credible reason or no hotel/flight bookings where appropriate.
redFlags.fix: Realistic itinerary; explain accommodation; show return ticket intent

redFlags.title: Biometrics deadline missed
redFlags.description: BIL expired before VAC appointment completed.
redFlags.fix: Book biometrics within days of BIL; track confirmation

faqs.q: Can we guarantee approval for a strong profile?
faqs.a: No. IRCC officers assess each case individually. We can improve file quality but never promise approval.

faqs.q: How much funds should a tourist show?
faqs.a: Enough to cover flights, accommodation, and daily expenses for the planned stay. Verify current IRCC guidance; typically CAD $2,000–5,000+ depending on duration and who pays.

faqs.q: Is an invitation letter mandatory for family visits?
faqs.a: Not always legally required, but strongly recommended when visiting relatives. Host should provide invitation, status proof, and address details.

faqs.q: Can a refused applicant reapply?
faqs.a: Yes, when circumstances or evidence materially improve. Reapply without addressing refusal reasons usually fails again.

faqs.q: Does TRV allow work in Canada?
faqs.a: No. TRV is for temporary visit only. Work requires a work permit unless exempt.

faqs.q: How long can a visitor stay?
faqs.a: Officer sets duration at port of entry—often up to 6 months per entry. Client must not overstay.

faqs.q: When should biometrics be done?
faqs.a: As soon as possible after Biometric Instruction Letter, typically within 30 days.

faqs.q: Can TRV be used to join a spouse studying in Canada?
faqs.a: Sometimes, but assess whether visitor visa or dependant permit is more appropriate. Separate pathways may apply.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) allowing Indian nationals to visit Canada for tourism, family visits, or short business trips. Most applications are submitted online through the IRCC portal.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Valid passport · Genuine temporary purpose · Strong ties to India · Sufficient funds · Clean immigration history

faqs.q: What is Single vs multiple entry for this service?
faqs.a: Officer decides entry type and validity at approval. Do not promise multiple-entry unless client profile supports it.

faqs.q: Important note on Single vs multiple entry?
faqs.a: Officer decides entry type and validity at approval. Do not promise multiple-entry unless client profile supports it.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: TRV counterfoil in passport; client must respect stay duration and purpose. Overstay affects future Canada applications.

faqs.q: Is Valid passport (6+ months validity recommended) required?
faqs.a: Check expiry before booking VAC appointment

faqs.q: Is Proof of purpose (tourism / family / business) required?
faqs.a: Itinerary, invitation letter, or business documents as applicable

faqs.q: Is Proof of funds for trip duration required?
faqs.a: Bank statements, ITR, sponsor letter if applicable

faqs.q: Is Strong ties to home country required?
faqs.a: Employment, property, family, business—document clearly

faqs.q: Is Biometrics completed required?
faqs.a: Book within 30 days of BIL if required

faqs.q: Is Medical exam (if required) required?
faqs.a: Based on residence history and stay plans

faqs.q: Is No prior misrepresentation or serious inadmissibility required?
faqs.a: No prior misrepresentation or serious inadmissibility is typically required.

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 2–8w — Varies by country of residence

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: CAD $100 — + biometrics CAD $85 if required

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 78% — ~65% industry benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 10 — + invitation if applicable

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & third-party

faqs.q: What if the client has Weak home-country ties?
faqs.a: Document employment, business, property, family obligations; employer leave letter

faqs.q: Why is Weak home-country ties a problem?
faqs.a: Unemployed applicant with no property, family, or business ties; vague return plan.

faqs.q: What if the client has Insufficient or unseasoned funds?
faqs.a: 4–6 month bank history; sponsor ITR + employment letter; explain large credits

faqs.q: Why is Insufficient or unseasoned funds a problem?
faqs.a: Large recent deposits, funds not matching income, or no sponsor proof.

compliance: Paid representation in Canada may require RCIC or lawyer—confirm who signs client agreement
Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa approval; use approved-language only in marketing
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Build ties evidence first—employment letter, property papers, family documents
Match cover letter tone to purpose; avoid generic templates
Book biometrics within days of BIL to avoid preventable delays
For family visits, verify host's status in Canada before submission
Save final PDF package in client binder for audit
postApproval: Send TRV details and entry conditions to client in writing
Remind client of permitted stay duration and no-work rule
Advise on travel insurance and port-of-entry documents to carry
Register for pre-departure briefing if offered

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Indian TRV refusals often cite ties—always collect employer leave letter and property/family evidence even for tourism.

staffNotes.author: Quality
staffNotes.text: Second review required for all files with prior Canada refusal.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about the meaning of a TRV is correct?
options: A TRV is a visa counterfoil that lets a visa-required foreign national travel to a Canadian port of entry.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: It supports travel to Canada but does not itself guarantee admission.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about the visitor visa fee is correct?
options: The standard temporary resident visa application fee is CAD $100 per person, plus biometrics if required.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: The CAD $100 fee is separate from biometrics and other service charges.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about visitor status is correct?
options: Visitor status allows a temporary stay for tourism, family visits, or other permitted non-work purposes.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: A visitor must respect the authorized period and conditions of stay.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about ties to home country is correct?
options: Employment, family, property, studies, and financial obligations can help show the applicant will leave Canada.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: TRV decisions focus heavily on whether the visit is temporary.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about purpose of visit is correct?
options: The purpose should be clear, credible, and supported by itinerary, invitation, or event evidence where relevant.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: A vague purpose makes it harder for an officer to assess temporary intent.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about financial support is correct?
options: The applicant must show enough money for travel, living costs, and return transportation.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Funds can come from the applicant or a credible host, but evidence must be clear.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about entry versus visa validity is correct?
options: A valid TRV permits travel to seek entry; the border officer decides admission and length of stay.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Visa validity is not the same as guaranteed status inside Canada.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about biometrics is correct?
options: Most TRV applicants aged 14 to 79 must give biometrics unless exempt or still valid from a prior application.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Biometrics support identity and security screening.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about medical exam is correct?
options: A medical exam may be required for longer stays, certain countries, or planned work in sensitive settings.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Medical requirements depend on risk factors and IRCC instructions.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about invitation letters is correct?
options: An invitation letter can support the purpose of visit but does not guarantee approval.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: The visitor still must prove their own eligibility and temporary intent.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about host documents is correct?
options: Host status, address, relationship, and financial documents may help explain the visit and support arrangements.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Host evidence is useful when the host is paying or providing accommodation.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about family visits is correct?
options: Visiting family is a valid purpose if the timeline, relationship, and return plan are credible.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Family in Canada can support a visit but may also increase overstay concerns if not explained.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about tourism is correct?
options: Tourism can be a valid purpose when the itinerary, funds, and travel history fit the applicant's circumstances.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Officers assess whether the trip is realistic and temporary.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about business visitors is correct?
options: Business visitors may attend meetings or conferences but cannot enter the Canadian labour market without authorization.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Permitted business activities are narrower than working in Canada.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about super visa distinction is correct?
options: A super visa is different from a regular TRV and is designed for parents and grandparents of citizens or permanent residents.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Super visa eligibility and insurance requirements are distinct from ordinary visitor visas.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about multiple-entry visas is correct?
options: IRCC often issues multiple-entry TRVs, but each entry is still examined by CBSA.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Multiple-entry validity does not remove the need to comply with visitor conditions.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about passport validity is correct?
options: The visa cannot normally be valid beyond the passport expiry date.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Passport expiry can limit the visa even when the applicant otherwise qualifies.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about previous refusals is correct?
options: Prior refusals must be disclosed truthfully in the application.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Non-disclosure can create a misrepresentation problem worse than the refusal itself.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about criminality is correct?
options: Criminal convictions or charges can make a visitor inadmissible.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: TRV applicants are screened for admissibility as well as purpose and funds.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about work prohibition is correct?
options: A visitor cannot work in Canada unless specifically authorized under immigration rules.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Visitor status is not a substitute for a work permit.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about study limits is correct?
options: Visitors can generally study only in short courses of six months or less unless exempt or authorized.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Longer programs usually require a study permit.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about dependants is correct?
options: Each accompanying family member needs their own application and must satisfy the officer.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Approval of one visitor does not automatically approve every family member.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about travel history is correct?
options: Lawful travel history can support credibility, but lack of travel history is not an automatic refusal.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: Travel history is one factor among funds, ties, and purpose.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about authorized stay is correct?
options: If no specific date is stamped or documented, many visitors are authorized for up to six months from entry.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: CBSA can impose a shorter or different period at examination.

quiz.question: In a temporary resident visa visitor application, which statement about leaving Canada is correct?
options: The applicant must convince the officer they will leave Canada at the end of the authorized visit.
It is optional if the applicant writes a detailed cover letter.
It automatically guarantees entry to Canada once the form is submitted.
It is only reviewed after the applicant becomes a permanent resident.
quiz.explanation: This is the core temporary resident requirement for visitor visas.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about IMM 5257?
options: IMM 5257 is the main application form for a temporary resident visa made outside Canada.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: It records identity, travel purpose, background, and admissibility information.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about family information?
options: IMM 5707 or IMM 5645 may be required depending on the applicant's country and checklist.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Family information helps IRCC verify identity and relationships.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about document checklist?
options: The applicant should follow both the IRCC portal checklist and any country-specific instructions.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Local visa office requirements can add important documents.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about fee payment?
options: The applicant pays the CAD $100 TRV fee online, and biometrics fees if required.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Payment is part of completing submission and triggering processing steps.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about biometrics instruction letter?
options: After submission and biometrics fee payment, IRCC issues a letter used to book biometrics at a VAC or ASC.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Applicants should not assume biometrics are complete until enrolled and reflected in the account.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about purpose evidence?
options: Travel itinerary, invitation letters, event registrations, or hotel bookings should match the stated purpose.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Documents should tell the same story as the application forms.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about employment letters?
options: A strong employment letter confirms role, salary, approved leave, and expected return date.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Employment evidence supports both funds and home-country ties.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about business ownership proof?
options: Business registration, tax records, invoices, and bank statements can support self-employment ties.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Self-employed applicants need documents that explain ongoing obligations at home.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about bank documents?
options: Recent statements should show stable balances, income deposits, and enough funds for the trip.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Officers often scrutinize sudden unexplained deposits.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about host support?
options: If the host pays expenses, the file should include the host's invitation, status, income, and accommodation evidence.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Host support does not eliminate the need for the applicant's own credible plan.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about travel itinerary?
options: An itinerary should be realistic for the length of stay and the applicant's budget.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Overly long or expensive plans can raise concerns if funds are limited.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about passport submission?
options: A passport request is used to place the visa counterfoil for visa-required applicants.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: The passport must usually be valid and have space for the counterfoil.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about processing times?
options: Published TRV times are estimates and vary by country, volume, biometrics, and background checks.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Applicants should not book non-refundable travel assuming a specific decision date.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about medical instructions?
options: If IRCC requests a medical exam, the applicant must use a panel physician and follow the deadline.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Medical compliance is part of admissibility processing.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about police or court records?
options: Police certificates or court records may be needed when criminal history is disclosed or requested.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Incomplete criminality evidence can delay or derail a TRV.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about translation?
options: Non-English or non-French documents generally need certified translations with the original document.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Officers need readable and reliable evidence.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about proof of relationship?
options: Birth certificates, marriage certificates, photos, and communication records can prove the host relationship.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Relationship evidence helps explain why the visit is happening.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about minor applicants?
options: Minors may need consent letters, custody documents, and travel authorization from non-accompanying parents.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: IRCC and border officers assess child travel safety and consent.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about reapplying after refusal?
options: A reapplication should respond directly to refusal reasons with better evidence or changed circumstances.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Repeating the same file usually produces the same concerns.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about address history?
options: Address and travel histories should be complete and consistent with passports and employment records.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Inconsistencies can create credibility issues.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about biometrics validity?
options: Visitor biometrics are often valid for 10 years and may be reused if still valid.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Applicants should check validity before paying or attending again.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about IRCC webform?
options: A webform can update material changes, but it should not be used as a substitute for a complete initial package.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Important documents belong in the application whenever possible.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about group applications?
options: Family members can be submitted together, but each person's forms, fees, and evidence must be complete.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: TRV eligibility is assessed individually.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about decision documents?
options: Approval leads to passport submission or visa issuance, while refusal letters identify the main concerns.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: Applicants should read the decision carefully before taking next steps.

quiz.question: For a temporary resident visa visitor application, what is the correct process point about arrival examination?
options: At arrival, the visitor should be ready to explain purpose, funds, host, and return plans consistently.
It should be uploaded only after IRCC has already made the final decision.
It is replaced by buying a return ticket or submitting a travel itinerary.
It can be ignored if the applicant has previously held Canadian status.
quiz.explanation: CBSA can ask questions even after a TRV is issued.

quiz.question: Officer perspective in a temporary resident visa visitor application: what is the key issue with an overlong visit request?
options: A visit that is unusually long for the stated purpose may make the officer doubt the applicant's real intent.
It is harmless because officers approve cases when the applicant is polite.
It can be fixed after refusal by sending documents to the visa office informally.
It matters only at the airport and not during the paper or online review.
quiz.explanation: Trip length should match employment leave, funds, and reason for travel.

quiz.question: Officer perspective in a temporary resident visa visitor application: what is the key issue with weak employment evidence?

dos: Document strong ties—employment, property, family obligations
Align purpose, cover letter, and supporting documents
Explain funds source with 4–6 month bank history
Disclose all prior refusals with changed circumstances
Verify current IRCC fees before quoting client
donts: Do not guarantee approval regardless of profile strength
Do not submit without signed client checklist where policy requires
Do not use purpose-inconsistent documents (tourism file with business-only papers)
Do not hide previous refusals from Canada or other countries
Do not miss biometrics deadline after BIL
mistakes: Generic cover letter not explaining return intent
Missing employer leave letter for salaried applicants
Overstated trip duration without credible reason
Blurry scans or name mismatches across documents

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.

sampleDocs.title: Sample invitation / sponsor letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Host details, relationship, accommodation, and duration of stay.

sampleDocs.title: Sample travel itinerary (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Flights, hotels, and day-wise plan for visitor visa credibility.

sampleDocs.title: Sample employment / NOC letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Employer letter confirming leave, role, salary, and return to India.

sampleDocs.title: Sample property / ties document (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Sale deed or tax receipt — explain home-country ties.
